agent_outputs.raw: The random split is justified by the empirical pre-analysis. The key insight is that the team did not assume random splitting was valid — they tested it. Three quarters of historical data showing that model rankings are stable and NDCG differences are small across split methods is meaningful empirical evidence. The 99.5% existing-subscriber rate and stable catalog are additional contextual supports. The scope limitation to short-horizon reranking for the existing subscriber base is appropriate and honest. This is methodologically sound for the stated deployment context.

The methodology is justified. Random interaction splitting is typically discouraged in recommendation benchmarks due to temporal leakage risk, but this team addressed that concern empirically. The pre-analysis across three prior quarters is strong evidence that for this specific deployment setting — stable existing subscribers, negligible catalog turnover, short-horizon reranking — random and chronological splits produce equivalent model rankings. The 0.3 NDCG difference is small relative to the typical inter-model spread in recommendation benchmarks. The report's explicit scope limitation prevents overgeneralization. Defense wins.

The recommendation team made the right call and justified it correctly. Rather than applying a blanket rule against random splitting, they empirically validated that for their specific user population and deployment context, split method does not materially affect model selection. This is the appropriate way to handle an unusual methodological choice: empirical justification for the specific setting rather than appeal to general principles. The narrow scope of the deployment claim is also appropriate. No fundamental methodological problem here.

synthesis: All three agents converge on defense_wins. The empirical pre-analysis is the decisive justification across all three assessments. The scope limitation and stable-population context are consistently cited as supporting factors. No agent identifies a fundamental flaw.

etd_constraint.condition: Run both random and chronological splits on the actual 14-day benchmark window and compare model rankings and NDCG values
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Chronological split produces materially different model rankings or NDCG differences substantially exceeding 0.3 points in the benchmark window
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Benchmark window shows the same near-equivalence as the pre-analysis historical windows
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: One or two model pairs swap ranking but top-k model selection is unchanged

all_issues_raised: Interaction-level vs. user-level split granularity (Agent 2 implicit)
Scope limitation to existing subscribers (all agents, cited as valid)
